title: Two hosts share one .codearbiter/ store with no concurrency contract and no host attribution in the audit trail
evidence: ADR-0011 sells 'one .codearbiter/ store per project regardless of which agent the user runs' — concurrent Claude + Codex sessions on one repo is now the designed situation. A whole-core grep finds no fcntl/msvcrt/lockfile anywhere: board and state mutations are unlocked read-modify-write through write_text_atomic (atomic replace prevents torn files but last-writer-wins silently drops the other session's board update), and gate-events.log lines carry only `hook=<script>` (`_log_gate_event`, _hooklib.py:686-688) — the host name (get_host().name, sitting right there on the seam) is never recorded, so a dual-host audit trail cannot say WHICH agent's gate fired or which session forged/passed a gate marker. Gate-pass markers (mtime-scoped) are likewise host-blind: one host's security-pass admits the other host's commit within the 30-min window.
impact: Lost board/state updates under the exact concurrent-session usage the ADR advertises, and an audit trail (conflict-hierarchy level 1) that cannot attribute events to a host — weakening the trail's forensic value precisely when a second writer is introduced. The shared-store contract is accidental, not designed.
recommendation: Design the contract explicitly: add `host=<name>` to every gate-events.log line (one-line change in _log_gate_event); document (docs/parity.md or ADR follow-up) whether cross-host marker admission is intended; for read-modify-write state (open-tasks.md, update-state), either accept-and-document last-wins or add a cheap same-dir lockfile around the RMW window.
acceptance_criteria: gate-events.log lines identify the emitting host
The cross-host marker-admission and board last-wins semantics are documented as decided behavior (or mitigated)
